description: Cross-paper edges for the local-Langlands-after-Fargues–Scholze DAG. from/to are '<arxiv_id>:<node_id>'; direction is subject-verb-object (from {type} to). The root node 2102.13459 (Fargues–Scholze) is SHARED with the perfectoid-spaces DAG — its per-paper DAG lives at perfectoid-spaces/papers/2102.13459.json. Downstream papers attach to thm:I.10.1 (the FS correspondence / packets $\Pi^{\mathrm{FS}}$), thm:I.9.6 (its properties), thm:I.8.1 (spectral action / moduli of $L$-parameters), thm:I.7.2 (Hecke functors), and thm:III.0.2 ($\mathrm{Bun}_G$ structure).

edges.note: Elliptic $\mathcal{E}$-stability is a statement about the Fargues–Scholze $L$-packets $\Pi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\varphi$; the virtual-character space the theorem ranges over is spanned by the characters of the packet the FS excursion-operator correspondence (thm:I.10.1) produces.
The spectral action used here is the Fargues–Scholze spectral action attached to the moduli of $L$-parameters (thm:I.8.1).

The $z$-embedding Hecke compatibility $T_V(\eta^*A) \simeq \eta^* T_{\widehat{\eta}^*V}(A)$ is proved by repeating Fargues–Scholze's Theorem IX.6.1 (the Hecke-functor formalism, thm:I.7.2) almost verbatim.
Hamann's $\phi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\pi$ (Thm 3.5) is the Fargues–Scholze parametrization built by the excursion-operator construction (thm:I.10.1); the whole compatibility theorem is about this map.

Hamann's Thm 3.6 quotes FS Thm I.9.6 verbatim — the properties (LCFT for tori, twist/contragredient/parabolic-induction compatibility) of the FS correspondence the proof exploits to force agreement with Gan–Takeda.
The Hecke operators $T_W$ on $D_{\mathrm{lis}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ (Thm 3.2) are imported from FS Thm I.7.2.
The spectral action / spectral Bernstein-center structure (Thm 3.7) is imported from the Fargues–Scholze spectral action attached to the moduli of $L$-parameters (thm:I.8.1).
The spectral action used to build the categorical functor is the Fargues–Scholze spectral action of $\mathrm{Perf}(Z^1(W_F,\widehat{G})/\widehat{G})$.
Fu's categorical equivalence $\Theta_0 = (-\ast W)$ verifies the categorical form of the FS correspondence (thm:I.10.1) for depth-zero regular supercuspidals.
Nguyen's Thm 2.1 quotes FS Thm III.0.2 — the structure of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ (small $v$-stack, points $\leftrightarrow B(G)$, HN strata) on which the categorical decomposition lives.
The properties of the FS correspondence $\pi \mapsto \phi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\pi$ (FS Thm I.9.6) pin the categorical decomposition to the correct $L$-parameters.
The Hecke eigensheaf $\mathcal{G}_\phi$ realizes the Fargues–Scholze correspondence (thm:I.10.1) as a case of the Fargues eigensheaf conjecture.
Scholze's motivic refinement reconstructs the Fargues–Scholze excursion correspondence (thm:I.10.1) over motivic coefficients, proving its independence of $\ell$ (FS Conjecture I.9.5).

The motivic geometric Satake equivalence (Thm 5.7) lifts the Fargues–Scholze Hecke/Satake construction (thm:I.7.2) to motivic coefficients, constructing the dual group and spectral action $\ell$-independently.
